methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $25M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $100M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 7.8x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

private.name: Zeta Surgical
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series A), post-money $100M

data.narrative:
Cellcolabs is a Series A biotech company founded by Mattias Bernow that has raised $25M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 8 private and 3 public companies in the Biotech & Health sector, including peers at Series B stage such as Eikon Therapeutics ($3.3B), Neko Health ($1.8B), New Limit ($810M), Asimov ($800M), and Profluent ($600M).

Two triangulation methods were applicable. Stage-progression analysis, using the $25M raised as a proxy for current valuation and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A advancement to the next stage, implies a valuation of $50M. Peer-median analysis across six same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables yields a median valuation of $805M, with a range spanning $100M to $3.3B. Public-comp anchoring was not applicable because the target's revenue remains undisclosed, which is typical for private companies and prevents meaningful P/S multiple application despite the sector median of 7.8x across three public tickers.

The two applicable methods produced materially divergent results. The triangulated range spans $42.5M (floor) to $926M (ceiling), with a median of $428M. This wide dispersion reflects the substantial stage and scale heterogeneity within the peer set. Key uncertainties include the timing and terms of Cellcolabs' prospective Series B, product-stage maturity relative to peers, and capital efficiency relative to comparable burn profiles.
